name: Qwen-72B
license: Tongyi Qianwen LICENSE AGREEMENT (custom, free commercial use below usage threshold)
archDisplay: Dense decoder-only transformer, 80 layers (RoPE, SwiGLU, RMSNorm)
note: Gen-1 flagship trained on 3T tokens with 32K context; beat Llama2-70B and topped GPT-3.5 on MMLU/C-Eval/GSM8K at release.
tpsNote: Q4 weights (~48GB) exceed a single RTX 4090's 24GB VRAM; requires multi-GPU or CPU/disk offload.

results.notes: 4-shot; from Qwen base-model comparison table on HF/GitHub README

LogicKor official leaderboard, model_id qwen/qwen-72b-chat, default prompt strategy [score corrected during adversarial verify: proposed 6.74 -> 6.73, per verifier: Fetched https://lk.instruct.kr/ directly. Confirmed model_id "qwen/qwen-72b-chat" exists on the LogicKor leaderboard with three rows by prompt strategy: default=6.73 (rank 104), 1-shot=7.75 (rank 67), cot-1-shot=7.42 (rank 82). The claim specifies "default prompt strategy," which is 6.73, not the claimed 6.74 (off by 0.01 — likely a rounding/transcription slip). Correctly matched to the older base Qwen-72B model (qwen-72b-chat), not conflated with the separate, higher-scoring Qwen2-72B-Instruct entries (8.61/8.69/8.46) — so no variant-conflation issue. No unit mismatch or wrong-model mixup found. Note: LogicKor site itself is archived/read-only since Oct 2024, but historical data remains accessible and unchanged on lk.instruct.kr.]

verificationNotes: Confirmed real, released 2023-11-30 per GitHub README. 72B params and 80 hidden layers confirmed via raw config.json (num_hidden_layers=80). Native 32K context (adapted RoPE base, no NTK extension needed) confirmed via GitHub README. Tongyi Qianwen commercial license confirmed. All 4 benchmark scores (mmlu 77.4, c-eval 83.3, gsm8k 78.9, humaneval 35.4) confirmed verbatim on HF/GitHub.
